pages.title: The Northern Cold
pages.text: Past the hundredth marker north, the air bites. Past the thousandth, no machine has yet flown back.$(br2)Cold thins lifting gas. Frost rimes propeller blades. Even Levitite, that defiant stone, yields to whatever cold hand grips this country.

pages.title: Reading the Toll
pages.text: Wear Engineer's Goggles to read the modifier on your machine.$(br2)$(thing)Cold Air Modifier$() reduces lift produced by a Hot Air Burner or Steam Vent.$(br2)$(thing)Frozen Blades Modifier$() reduces thrust produced by any propeller.$(br2)Levitite blocks dim with the same toll, though they wear no readout. Trust the propeller bearing's modifier as your gauge.$(br2)At $(c)-100%$(), no airborne craft of any design will hold its altitude.

pages.title: Building for the Cold
pages.text: There is no insulation that defeats this climate. Heavier engines do not push harder. Larger envelopes do not lift more. The cold simply takes its share.$(br2)What works is redundancy. Bring spare envelopes. Bring three times the fuel you think you need. Plan landings before each leg, not when the altimeter starts to fall.$(br2)The cold respects preparation. Nothing else.
